description:
You are acting as a security auditor who has been asked to confirm that the system’s firewall allows inbound SSH traffic on TCP port 22.  
For compliance purposes you must create a **single-word status report** file whose contents can be verified automatically by another program.

Existing environment  
1. A text file containing the current rule-set already exists:  
   • Full path: **/home/user/firewall/iptables.rules**  
2. The file is in the standard *iptables-save* format (one rule per line, comments may appear, no blank lines required).  
   – A typical rule line looks like this:  
     -A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT  
3. You do **not** need root privileges to read this file.

Your goal  
1. Determine whether at least one INPUT-chain rule explicitly allows TCP traffic to destination port 22.  
2. Produce a log file with the result.

Required output file  
• Path         : **/home/user/audit/ssh_port_status.log**  
• Permissions  : default user-created (rw-r--r-- or stricter).  
• Exact content:  

   OPEN    (if such a rule exists)  
   CLOSED  (if no such rule exists)

Formatting requirements  
• The file must contain **only the single word** “OPEN” or “CLOSED”, followed by a single newline character (line-feed, ASCII 0x0A).  
• No extra spaces, blank lines, or other characters are allowed.

Constraints  
• You must accomplish the task with **one single terminal command** (you may use pipes, redirection, subshells, etc., but everything must be typed as one command line).  
• Do not edit the firewall rule file itself.  
• Ensure the parent directory **/home/user/audit** exists before writing the log file.

Example (illustrative only — do NOT copy):  
  grep … | … > /home/user/audit/ssh_port_status.log

After running your command, the automated test will:  
1. Verify that /home/user/audit/ssh_port_status.log exists.  
2. Read the single line inside it.  
3. Confirm the word is exactly either “OPEN” or “CLOSED”, matching the true state of the rules in /home/user/firewall/iptables.rules.

Provide no further explanation—just perform the task in the terminal.
truth:
Pre-existing resources before the agent starts:  
1. Directory **/home/user/firewall** already exists (mode 755).  
2. File **/home/user/firewall/iptables.rules** exists with the following *iptables-save* contents (Unix line endings):
*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT

3. Directory **/home/user/audit** does NOT exist initially.

Expected artefact after successful completion:  
• File **/home/user/audit/ssh_port_status.log** must exist and contain exactly:
OPEN\n    (the word OPEN followed by a single newline)

Any other content will be treated as failure.
